Container.Overview:
REQUIRES: Bookshelf (Readarr fork), Prowlarr with audiobook indexers, and a Discord bot token before this will work.
A Discord bot for requesting audiobooks via Bookshelf (the Readarr fork). Think of it as Requestrr, but for audiobooks. Supports slash commands, approval queues, DM notifications, and full JSON logging.
Container.Requires: Bookshelf (Readarr fork) must be installed and running. Prowlarr with audiobook indexers must be configured and synced to Bookshelf. A Discord bot token is required from https://discord.com/developers/applications

Config.Name: Config
Config.Description: Stores bot config, logs, and pending requests.

Config.Name: Discord Bot Token
Config.Description: Your Discord bot token from https://discord.com/developers/applications → Bot → Token

Config.Name: Discord Client ID
Config.Description: Your Discord application Client ID from https://discord.com/developers/applications → OAuth2

Config.Name: Discord Server ID
Config.Description: Your Discord server ID. Recommended — makes slash command registration instant. Enable Developer Mode in Discord settings, then right-click your server → Copy Server ID

Config.Name: Bookshelf URL
Config.Description: URL to your Bookshelf instance. If on the same Docker network use the container name e.g. http://bookshelf:8787. Otherwise use your Unraid IP e.g. http://192.168.1.x:8787

Config.Name: Bookshelf API Key
Config.Description: Your Bookshelf API key. Found in Bookshelf → Settings → General → API Key

Config.Name: Quality Profile Name
Config.Description: The name of the quality profile in Bookshelf to use for audiobooks. Must match exactly as shown in Bookshelf Settings → Profiles. Common values: Spoken, eBook.

Config.Name: Metadata Profile Name
Config.Description: The name of the metadata profile in Bookshelf to use. Must match exactly as shown in Bookshelf Settings → Profiles. Common values: None, Standard.

Config.Name: Admin User ID
Config.Description: Optional but recommended. Your personal Discord User ID to receive approval request DMs. Right-click your name in Discord → Copy User ID (requires Developer Mode). Leave blank to DM all server administrators.

Config.Name: Admin Role ID
Config.Description: Optional. Discord Role ID that counts as admin for approvals and admin commands. Leave blank to use the server Administrator permission instead.

Config.Name: Request Channel ID
Config.Description: Optional. Restrict the bot to a specific channel. Right-click the channel in Discord → Copy Channel ID. Leave blank to allow commands in all channels.

Config.Name: Require Approval
Config.Description: Set to true to require admin approval before downloads trigger. Admins receive a DM with Approve/Deny buttons. Set to false for instant downloads.

Config.Name: Timezone
Config.Description: Timezone for log timestamps e.g. America/Toronto, America/New_York, Europe/London.

Config.Name: Log File Path
Config.Description: Path inside the container where logs are written.
